title: Decompose fractions visually
description: Practice using visuals to decompose fractions.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the number line that represents the equation, $\dfrac15+\dfrac15+\dfrac15+\dfrac15=\dfrac45$.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The equation uses fifths. So we need a number line where each whole is divided into $5$ equal parts. 



[[☃ image 1]]

In the equation, we add $\dfrac15+\dfrac15+\dfrac15+\dfrac15$. So we need to move $\dfrac15$ four times.



[[☃ image 1]]

This number line represents the equation:


[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: ![A number line labeled 0 to 1 with tick marks every one-fifth unit marked. An arrow begins at 0 and moves right one tick mark, then moves right one tick mark, then moves right one tick mark, and then moves right one tick mark, ending at the fourth tick mark to the right of 0.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/72a00ad8277bcd62dca371f0c127fa35f444c50a)

![A number line labeled 0 to 1 with tick marks every one-sixth unit marked. An arrow begins at 0 and moves right one tick mark, then right one tick mark, then right one tick mark, and then right one tick mark, ending at the fourth tick mark to the right of 0.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/041eac27ea8e488a97bc788818a459458a661bbb)

![A number line labeled 0 to 1 with tick marks every one-sixth unit marked. An arrow begins at 0 and moves right one tick mark, then right one tick mark, then right one tick mark, then right one tick mark, and then right one tick mark, ending at the fifth tick mark to the right of 0.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b3ef5ae0150017a813a361e391c13992af12da16)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the number line that represents the equation, $\dfrac48+\dfrac18+\dfrac28=\dfrac78$.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The equation uses eighths. So we need a number line where each whole is divided into $8$ equal parts. 



[[☃ image 1]]

In the equation, we add $\dfrac48+\dfrac18+\dfrac28$. 



[[☃ image 1]]

This number line represents the equation:


[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: ![A number line labeled 0 to 1 with tick marks every one-eighth unit marked. An arrow begins at 0 and moves right 4 tick marks, then moves right 2 tick marks, then moves right 1 tick mark, ending at the seventh tick mark to the right of 0.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a9e0fab07e5d7db8c4b1568b79b517b8347680ae)

![A number line labeled 0 to 1 with tick marks every one-tenth unit marked. An arrow begins at 0 and moves right 4 tick marks, then moves right 2 tick marks, then moves right 1 tick mark, ending at the seventh tick mark to the right of 0.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7670b5e3c2900c50fbaac57d81dcdbada2ed861c)

![A number line labeled 0 to 1 with tick marks every one-eighth unit marked. An arrow begins at 0 and moves right 3 tick marks, then moves right 2 tick marks, then moves right 1 tick mark, ending at the sixth tick mark to the right of 0.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5bf5d09c62a8ee19b27cc802d9a9484f9fb94caa)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the number line that represents the equation, $\dfrac26+\dfrac36+\dfrac26=\dfrac76$.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The equation uses sixths. So we need a number line where each whole is divided into $6$ equal parts. 



[[☃ image 1]]



In the equation, we add $\dfrac26+\dfrac36+\dfrac26$. 



[[☃ image 1]]

This number line represents the equation:


[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: ![A number line labeled 0 to 2 with tick marks at 0, 1, and 2 emphasized. Tick marks at every one-sixth unit are marked. An arrow begins at 0 and moves right two tick marks, then moves right three tick marks, then moves right two tick marks, ending at the first tick mark to the right of 1.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/25b1dc6fae2af344bacc8d1178c66dc17f65a899)

![A number line labeled 0 to 2 with tick marks at 0, 1, and 2 emphasized. Tick marks at every one-eighth unit are marked. An arrow begins at 0 and moves right two tick marks, then moves right three tick marks, then moves right two tick marks, ending at the seventh tick mark to the right of 0.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/50da5f00af34a5367f0e982230a985541cd43f59)

![A number line labeled 0 to 2 with tick marks at 0, 1, and 2 emphasized. Tick marks at every one-fourth unit are marked. An arrow begins at 0 and moves right two tick marks, then moves right three tick marks, them moves right two tick marks, ending at the third tick mark to the right of 1.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5daa5e0f1d18128f25b38d360f6740567d70670d)

assessment_items.question_content:
Each tape diagram represents $1$ whole.

**Choose the tape diagram that represents the equation, $\dfrac18+\dfrac28+\dfrac28=\dfrac58$.**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
The equation uses eighths. So we need a tape diagram with $8$ equal parts. 


[[☃ image 1]]

In the equation, we add $\dfrac{1}{8}$, $\dfrac{2}{8}$, and $\dfrac{2}{8}$. So we need groups of $1$ eighth, $2$ eighths, and another $2$ eighths.



[[☃ image 1]]

Altogether, the groups show $5$ eighths.
This tape diagram represents the equation:



[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: ![A tape diagram divided into 8 equal parts.  The first part is shaded purple with a curved bracket above it labeled question mark. The second and third parts are shaded pink with a curved bracket above them labeled question mark. The fourth and fifth parts are shaded green with a curved bracket above them labeled question mark. Together, the curved brackets above the shaded parts are labeled question mark plus question mark plus question mark.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/012931d566d4ce62a649be9dd0ea2f89ab2048ea)

![A tape diagram divided into 9 equal parts. The first part is shaded purple with a curved bracket above it labeled question mark. The second and third parts are shaded pink with a curved bracket above them labeled question mark. The fourth and fifth parts are shaded green with a curved bracket above them labeled question mark. Together the curved brackets above the shaded parts are labeled question mark plus question mark plus question mark.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/fdea2ada81b0b1d26aa4dfc20e3af23217ec5b68)

![A tape diagram divided into 6 equal parts. The first part is shaded purple with a curved bracket above it labeled question mark. The second and third parts are shaded pink with a curved bracket above them labeled question mark. The fourth and fifth parts are shaded green with a curved bracket above them labeled question mark. Together, the curved brackets above the shaded parts are labeled question mark plus question mark plus question mark.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7d7c58f8cd69b286de2cdf5b5d1db048f666add9)

assessment_items.question_content:
Each tape diagram represents $1$ whole.

**Choose the tape diagram that represents the equation, $\dfrac{2}{10}+\dfrac3{10}+\dfrac{1}{10}=\dfrac6{10}$.**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
The equation uses tenths. So we need a tape diagram with $10$ equal parts. 



[[☃ image 1]]

In the equation, we add $\dfrac{2}{10}$, $\dfrac{3}{10}$, and $\dfrac{1}{10}$. So we need groups of $2$ tenths, $3$ tenths, and another $1$ tenth.



[[☃ image 1]]

Altogether, the groups show $6$ tenths.
This tape diagram represents the equation:



[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: ![A tape diagram divided into 10 equal parts. The first and second parts are shaded purple with a curved bracket above them labeled question mark. The third, fourth, and fifth parts are shaded pink with a curved bracket above them labeled question mark. The sixth part is labeled green with a curved bracket above it labeled question mark. Together the curved brackets above the shaded part is labeled question mark plus question mark plus question mark.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/336dfd15d7bf620b92cd9c56b6420fe52ff32230)

![A tape diagram divided into 12 equal parts. The first and second parts are shaded purple with a curved bracket above them labeled question mark. The third, fourth, and fifth parts are shaded pink with a curved bracket above them labeled question mark. The sixth part is labeled green with a curved bracket above it labeled question mark. Together the curved brackets above the shaded part is labeled question mark plus question mark plus question mark.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ce7df2cfa87f73eaa7baeaf05dd1e94cc678a517)

![A tape diagram divided into 8 equal parts. The first and second parts are shaded purple with a curved bracket above them labeled question mark. The third, fourth, and fifth parts are shaded pink with a curved bracket above them labeled question mark. The sixth part is labeled green with a curved bracket above it labeled question mark. Together the curved brackets above the shaded part is labeled question mark plus question mark plus question mark.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e7775af32068ac9be8098e5a08d3e7c634f744a7)

assessment_items.question_content:
Each tape diagram represents $1$ whole.

**Choose the tape diagram that represents the equation, $\dfrac{1}{4}+\dfrac2{4}=\dfrac3{4}$.**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
The equation uses fourths. So we need a tape diagram with $4$ equal parts. 



[[☃ image 1]]

In the equation, we add $\dfrac{1}{4}$ and $\dfrac{2}{4}$. So we need groups of $1$ fourth and $2$ fourths.


[[☃ image 1]]

Altogether, the groups show $3$ fourths.
This tape diagram represents the equation:



[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: ![A tape diagram divided into 4 equal parts. The first part is shaded purple with a curved bracket above it labeled question mark. The second and third parts are shaded pink with a curved bracket above them labeled question mark. Together the curved brackets above the shaded parts are labeled question mark plus question mark.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8cda711a73567b4a9dd83109612f0d8089d99f24)

![A tape diagram divided into 8 equal parts. The first part is shaded purple with a curved bracket above it labeled question mark. The second and third parts are shaded pink with a curved bracket above them labeled question mark. The fourth, fifth, and sixth parts are shaded green with a curved bracket above it labeled question mark. Together the curved brackets above the shaded parts are labeled question mark plus question mark plus question mark.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/51f86389d3d76a48aaaec29b3fbe829273bb3c99)

![A tape diagram divided into 6 equal parts. The first part is shaded purple with a curved bracket above it labeled question mark. The second and third parts are shaded pink with a curved bracket above labeled question mark. Together the curved brackets above the shaded parts are labeled question mark plus question mark.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7cfcf103ed15cba83b367fd73881059a834ec3f8)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the equation represented by the following number line.**

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
There are $4$ equal parts in $1$ whole, so each segment is equal to $\dfrac14$.

[[☃ image 1]]

Each segment is $\dfrac14$, so we can count the number of segments to find the size of each jump .


[[☃ image 1]]

The equation represented by the number line is $\dfrac14+\dfrac34+\dfrac24=\dfrac64$.

options.alt: A number line labeled 0 to 2 with tick marks 0, 1, and 2 emphasized. Tick marks every one-fourth unit are marked. An arrow begins at 0 and moves right 1 tick mark, then moves right 3 tick marks, and then moves right 2 tick marks, ending two tick marks to the right of 1.

choices.content: $\dfrac14+\dfrac34+\dfrac24=\dfrac64$
$\dfrac16+\dfrac36+\dfrac26=\dfrac66$
$\dfrac11+\dfrac13+\dfrac12=\dfrac36$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the equation represented by the following number line.**


[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
There are $10$ equal parts in $1$ whole, so each segment is equal to $\dfrac1{10}$.


[[☃ image 1]]

Each segment is $\dfrac1{10}$, so we can count the number of segments to find the size of each jump .



[[☃ image 1]]

The equation represented by the number line is $\dfrac3{10}+\dfrac3{10}+\dfrac2{10} = \dfrac8{10}$.

options.alt: A number line labeled 0 to 1 with tick marks at every one-tenth unit marked. An arrow beings at 0 and moves right three tick marks, then moves right three tick marks, and then moves right two tick marks, ending at the eighth tick mark to the right of 0.

choices.content: $\dfrac3{10}+\dfrac3{10}+\dfrac2{10} = \dfrac8{10}$
$\dfrac38+\dfrac38+\dfrac28=\dfrac88$
$\dfrac3{10}+\dfrac3{10}+\dfrac2{10}=\dfrac2{10}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the equation represented by the following number line.**

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
There are $3$ equal parts in $1$ whole, so each segment is equal to $\dfrac1{3}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

Each segment is $\dfrac1{3}$, so we can count the number of segments to find the size of each jump .

[[☃ image 1]]

The equation represented by the number line is $\dfrac1{3}+\dfrac3{3}+\dfrac1{3} = \dfrac5{3}$.

options.alt: A number line labeled 0 to 2 with tick marks at 0, 1, and 2 emphasized. Tick marks at every one-third unit are marked. An arrow begins at 0 and moves right one tick mark, then moves right three tick marks, then moves right one tick mark, ending at the fifth tick mark to the right of 0.

choices.content: $\dfrac1{3}+\dfrac3{3}+\dfrac1{3} = \dfrac5{3}$
$\dfrac1{6}+\dfrac3{6}+\dfrac1{6} = \dfrac5{6}$
$\dfrac1{3}+\dfrac2{3}+\dfrac1{3} = \dfrac4{3}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the equation represented by the following tape diagram.**  
*The entire tape diagram represents $1$ whole.*



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
There are $6$ equal parts in the whole, so each segment is $\dfrac16$.



[[☃ image 1]]

The diagram shows segments that are equal to $\dfrac16 + \dfrac26 + \dfrac26$.


[[☃ image 1]]



Altogether, the groups show $\dfrac56$.
The equation represented by the tape diagram is $\dfrac16+\dfrac26 + \dfrac26=\dfrac56$.

options.alt: A tape diagram divided into 6 equal parts. The first part is shaded purple with a curved bracket above it labeled question mark. The second and third parts are shaded pink with a curved bracket above them labeled question mark. The fourth and fifth parts are shaded green with a curved bracket above them labeled question mark. Together the curved brackets above the shaded parts are labeled question mark plus question mark plus question mark.

choices.content: $\dfrac16+\dfrac26 + \dfrac26=\dfrac56$
$\dfrac15+\dfrac25+\dfrac25=\dfrac5{10}$
$\dfrac16+\dfrac25+ \dfrac23=\dfrac5{14}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the equation represented by the following tape diagram.**  
*The entire tape diagram represents $1$ whole.*


[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
There are $5$ equal parts in the whole, so each segment is $\dfrac15$.



[[☃ image 1]]

The diagram shows segments that are equal to $\dfrac25 + \dfrac15 + \dfrac15$.


[[☃ image 1]]



Altogether, the groups show $\dfrac45$.
The equation represented by the tape diagram is $\dfrac25+\dfrac15 + \dfrac15=\dfrac45$.

options.alt: A tape diagram divided into 5 equal parts. The first and second parts are shaded purple with a curved bracket labeled question mark above them. The third part is shaded pink with a curved bracket labeled question mark above it. The fourth part is shaded green with a curved bracket labeled question mark above it. Together the curved brackets above the shaded parts are labeled question mark plus question mark plus question mark.

choices.content: $\dfrac25+\dfrac15 + \dfrac15=\dfrac45$
$\dfrac16+\dfrac26+\dfrac26=\dfrac5{6}$
$\dfrac25+\dfrac13+ \dfrac12=\dfrac4{10}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the equation represented by the following tape diagram.**  
*The entire tape diagram represents $1$ whole.*

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
There are $12$ equal parts in the whole, so each segment is $\dfrac1{12}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

The diagram shows segments that are equal to $\dfrac4{12} + \dfrac1{12} + \dfrac5{12}$.

[[☃ image 1]]



Altogether, the groups show $\dfrac{10}{12}$.
The equation represented by the tape diagram is $\dfrac4{12}+\dfrac1{12} + \dfrac5{12}=\dfrac{10}{12}$.

options.alt: A tape diagram divided into 12 equal segments. The first 4 segments are shaded purple with a curved bracket above them labeled question mark. The fifth segment is shaded pink and labeled question mark. Segments 6 through 10 are shaded green with a curved bracket above them labeled question mark. Together the curved brackets above the tape diagram are labeled question mark plus question mark plus question mark.

choices.content: $\dfrac4{12}+\dfrac1{12}+ \dfrac4{12}=\dfrac9{12}$
